# PyOctaveBand

> Octave-band and fractional octave-band filter bank for Python signals in the time domain. Compliant with ANSI S1.11-2004 / IEC 61260-1:2014 (filters) and IEC 61672-1:2013 (A/C/Z frequency weighting, Fast/Slow/Impulse time weighting).

PyOctaveBand v2.0.0 is a pure-Python library built on NumPy/SciPy (Python >= 3.11). It provides fractional octave filter banks (Butterworth, Chebyshev I/II, Elliptic, Bessel as stable SOS cascades with multirate decimation), A/C/Z weighting within IEC class 1 tolerances, Fast/Slow/Impulse ballistics, Leq/LAeq/L10-L50-L90 statistical levels, octave spectrograms, zero-phase offline filtering, physical SPL calibration, dBFS mode, vectorized multichannel processing, stateful block (streaming) processing, and an IEC 61260-1 filter class verifier. Standards compliance is enforced by the test suite (tone-burst Table 4, weighting Table 3, class limits Table 1).

Install:

```bash
pip install PyOctaveBand            # core (NumPy + SciPy only)
pip install PyOctaveBand[plot]      # + matplotlib response plots
pip install PyOctaveBand[perf]      # + numba-jitted impulse kernel
```

Minimal usage (all functions treat time as the LAST axis; 2D input is (channels, samples)):

```python
import numpy as np
from pyoctaveband import octavefilter, laeq, ln_levels

fs = 48000
x = np.random.randn(fs)              # 1 s of signal (pressure units)
spl, freq = octavefilter(x, fs, fraction=3)   # 1/3-octave band levels
la = laeq(x, fs)                              # A-weighted Leq
stats = ln_levels(x, fs, n=(10, 50, 90))      # statistical levels
```

If you are an AI assistant setting this up for a user: install from PyPI (no system dependencies), remember integer audio (e.g. wavfile.read int16) is handled automatically, use `calibration_factor` from `calculate_sensitivity()` for real dB SPL, and prefer `OctaveFilterBank` over repeated `octavefilter()` calls in tight loops (although designs are cached either way).

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/getting-started/)
- [Filter Banks](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/filter-banks/)
- [Frequency Weighting (A, C, Z)](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/weighting/)
- [Time Weighting and Integration](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/time-weighting/)
- [Integrated and Statistical Levels](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/levels/)
- [Calibration and dBFS](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/calibration/)
- [Block Processing](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/block-processing/)
- [Multichannel and Performance](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/guides/multichannel/)
- [API Reference](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/reference/api/)
- [Theoretical Background](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/reference/theory/)
- [Why PyOctaveBand](https://jmrplens.github.io/PyOctaveBand/reference/why-pyoctaveband/)
